# @zakkster/lite-headless / color-picker

Headless color picker. Internal state is HSV; public reads and writes
support HSV, sRGB, HEX, HSL, and OKLCH (the latter first-class because
the @zakkster design-system stack lives in OKLCH).

## What you get

```js
import { createColorPicker } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/color-picker";

const cp = createColorPicker({
    defaultHex: "#7dd3fc",
    alpha: true,                  // default; pass false to disable alpha
    onValueChange: (hsv, reason) => { /* state, "drag-area"|"setHue"|... */ },
    onCommit:      (hsv, reason) => { /* on drag-end / swatch click */ },
});

cp.attachRoot(rootEl);
cp.attachArea(areaEl);                 // 2D saturation × brightness
cp.attachAreaHandle(areaHandleEl);
cp.attachHueSlider(hueRailEl);         // 1D hue rail (default horizontal)
cp.attachHueHandle(hueHandleEl);
cp.attachAlphaSlider(alphaRailEl);     // 1D alpha rail (optional)
cp.attachAlphaHandle(alphaHandleEl);
cp.attachSwatch(swatchBtn, "#ff5500"); // preset color
```

## Reads (call-style accessors)

- `cp.hue()`         → 0..360
- `cp.saturation()`  → 0..1
- `cp.brightness()`  → 0..1 (the V in HSV)
- `cp.alpha()`       → 0..1
- `cp.hsv()`         → `{ h, s, v, a }`
- `cp.rgb()`         → `{ r, g, b, a }` (r/g/b are 0..255 integers)
- `cp.hex()`         → `"#rrggbb"` or `"#rrggbbaa"` if alpha < 1
- `cp.hsl()`         → `{ h, s, l, a }`
- `cp.oklch()`       → `{ l, c, h, a }` (l ~ 0..1, c ~ 0..0.4, h 0..360)

## Writes (mutations)

- `cp.setHue(n, reason?)`         — 0..360 with wraparound
- `cp.setSaturation(n, reason?)`  — clamped to [0, 1]
- `cp.setBrightness(n, reason?)`  — clamped to [0, 1]
- `cp.setAlpha(n, reason?)`       — clamped to [0, 1]
- `cp.setHsv({h,s,v,a}, reason?)` — partial fields allowed
- `cp.setRgb({r,g,b,a}, reason?)` — r/g/b in 0..255
- `cp.setHex(s, reason?)`         — returns `true` on success, `false` on parse error
- `cp.setOklch({l,c,h,a}, reason?)` — out-of-gamut silently clamped

Every setter calls `onValueChange` if and only if the underlying signal
actually changed (no-op writes are filtered). The `reason` channel
distinguishes drag-area / drag-hue / drag-alpha / setHue / setRgb /
setHex / setOklch / swatch / attribute / api.

## Painted CSS custom properties

Painted on attached elements; consumer CSS reads them to render gradients
and handle positions. Per the @zakkster/lite-headless CSS contract,
state lives in data-attributes; positions and color values live in
custom properties.

**Root** (`[data-color-picker-root]`):
- `--color-hex`         — current hex (e.g. `#7dd3fc`)
- `--color-h`           — hue 0..360
- `--color-s`           — saturation 0..1
- `--color-v`           — brightness 0..1
- `--color-r/g/b`       — sRGB channels 0..255
- `--color-a`           — alpha 0..1

**Area** (`[data-color-area]`):
- `--color-h`           — hue of the area background
- `--saturation`        — 0..1 (current x position)
- `--brightness`        — 0..1 (current y position; 1 = top)

**Area handle** (`[data-color-area-handle]`):
- `--x`                 — saturation, 0..1 (left-to-right)
- `--y`                 — 1 - brightness, 0..1 (top-to-bottom)

**Hue rail** (`[data-color-hue-slider]` and `[data-color-hue-handle]`):
- `--hue-pct`           — 0..1 (hue / 360)

**Alpha rail** (`[data-color-alpha-slider]` and `[data-color-alpha-handle]`):
- `--color-hex`         — solid color behind the alpha rail
- `--alpha`             — 0..1

## Wrapper (`<lite-color-picker>`)

```html
<lite-color-picker value="#7dd3fc">
    <div data-color-area>
        <div data-color-area-handle></div>
    </div>
    <div data-color-hue-slider>
        <div data-color-hue-handle></div>
    </div>
    <div data-color-alpha-slider>
        <div data-color-alpha-handle></div>
    </div>
    <button data-color-swatch data-color="#ff5500">red</button>
    <button data-color-swatch data-color="#00aaff">cyan</button>
</lite-color-picker>
```

Reactive attributes:
- `value="#rrggbb"` — bidirectional; external `setAttribute("value", ...)` flows in via `setHex`.
- `alpha="false"`   — disable the alpha channel; alpha attach helpers no-op.

Host accessors (canonical):
- `host.hex`, `host.rgb`, `host.hsv`, `host.hsl`, `host.oklch`  (read; pure objects)
- `host.hue`, `host.saturation`, `host.brightness`, `host.alpha` (numbers)
- `host.setHex(s)`, `host.setRgb(o)`, `host.setHsv(o)`, `host.setOklch(o)`, `host.setAlpha(n)`
- `host._colorPickerInstance` (advanced)

Events (bubbles up from host):
- `valuechange` — `{ detail: { hsv, hex, rgb, oklch, reason } }`
- `commit`      — same shape, on drag-end / swatch click

## OKLCH first-class

`oklch()` returns `{ l, c, h, a }` with the spec-defined ranges. Setter
accepts the same shape. The conversion goes through linear-sRGB +
OKLab (per CSS Color 4) and is round-trip stable to within 1 sRGB
unit per channel. Out-of-gamut OKLCH values are clamped at the sRGB
boundary; callers wanting gamut mapping should pre-process.

## Zero-alloc hot path

Pointer drag on the area and on the rails does NOT allocate per-frame:
- Track rect cached on `pointerdown`, invalidated on `scroll` / `resize`
  / `pointerup` / `pointercancel`.
- `pointerToSV` / `pointerToValue` read the cached rect and call the
  setter directly with primitive numbers.
- Signal updates use call-style accessors with the existing dirty-check
  in `_setIfDifferent` so a pixel move that doesn't cross a value
  boundary still emits zero allocations downstream.

CSS-custom-property writes use `style.setProperty(...)` with `toFixed(4)`
strings; this does allocate a small string per write, but only when
the signal value actually changed (the effect's dependency graph
ensures we don't re-paint unchanged elements).

## Composition

```js
import { createColorPicker } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/color-picker";
import { createFormField }   from "@zakkster/lite-headless/form-field";
import { effect }            from "@zakkster/lite-signal";

const ff = createFormField({ defaultRequired: true });
const cp = createColorPicker({ defaultHex: "#7dd3fc" });

// Bridge: the hex string is the field's "value"; validate it.
effect(() => {
    const hex = cp.hex();
    const ok = /^#[0-9a-f]{6}([0-9a-f]{2})?$/i.test(hex);
    ff.setValid(ok, ok ? null : "Invalid hex");
});
```

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
